page: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor. The CPU allocation is dedicated, providing 12 vCPUs across 6 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The processor features a 38 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI instruction sets. It is equipped with 170 GB of system memory, yielding 14.17 GB per core.

This server features a bundled NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 80 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is provided via a 402 GB NVMe SSD. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in this configuration.

In benchmark testing, the server exhibits highly polarized performance characteristics. CPU-bound single-core tasks, such as single-threaded gzip compression and single-core stress-ng tests, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the middle 50% tier. Conversely, the server achieves top-tier performance in large language model inference benchmarks, demonstrating high throughput for both prompt processing and text generation across 135M, 7B, and 70B parameter models.

Given its hardware profile, the server offers high resource density for GPU-intensive tasks, balancing the cost of a high-capacity 80 GB VRAM accelerator with a moderate 12-vCPU host configuration. This makes it highly cost-efficient for workloads that bottleneck on GPU memory rather than raw host CPU compute. The server is primarily suited for LLM inference, deep learning model execution, and other GPU-accelerated data science workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for GPU-intensive workloads. It features an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 12 dedicated vCPUs, 6 physical cores, and a 2.2 GHz clock speed, paired with 170 GB of system memory. Acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 80 GB of VRAM, supported by a local 402 GB NVMe SSD. While single-core CPU benchmarks and text processing fall into the lower performance tiers, the server delivers average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance. Crucially, it achieves top-tier, top 10% performance in LLM inference benchmarks across small, medium, and large models. This configuration provides a cost-efficient balance of massive GPU memory and moderate host CPU resources, making it highly suited for machine learning inference, deep learning, and GPU-accelerated data science tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 12 vCPUs, 170 GB RAM, a 402 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 80 GB VRAM for top-tier LLM inference performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g features 12 vCPUs, 170 GB RAM, a 402 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA A100 GPU for high-performance LLM inference.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized server combining an NVIDIA A100 GPU with high-capacity system memory for top-tier machine learning and inference workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with one NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU featuring 80 GB of VRAM
Features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 170 GB of system memory
Includes a local 402 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed storage
Delivers top-tier, top 10% performance in LLM inference benchmarks
Best suited for deep learning, LLM inference, and GPU-accelerated data science
